Yo builders! 🚀
I just open-sourced a self-evolving AI Agent Skill to manage your X (Twitter) presence directly from your terminal or IDE (works with OpenCode, Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Gemini CLI).
Here’s the cool part: It actually executes the work. 
Instead of just generating text to copy-paste, the agent uses a CLI to read your X feed, pull competitor posts, draft tailored replies, and post directly. It does the heavy lifting on its own — all it needs is your "yes" (explicit approval) to post or reply!
How onboarding works:
On first run, the agent interviews you, automatically installs twitter-cli, analyzes your X history/replies to extract your authentic voice, and auto-generates your customized 90-day growth strategy.
What it does during your coding sessions:
* 🔍 Feed & Trend Scanning: Scans your algorithmic feed and presents structured Reply Cards (Post, Strategy, Draft). You just review and approve to post instantly.
* 🎬 Niche-Engineered Posts: Drafts hook-first posts from your actual coding assets (repos, screenshots, visual hooks) and schedules or posts them directly on approval.
* 💬 Authentic Voice Matching: Drafts replies matching your real voice. Uses a strict Voice Anti-Patterns Checklist to kill obvious AI-isms (no "Great take! 🔥" or "This is a game-changer").
* 🧠 Self-Evolving Memory: Upgrades its own strategy files, re-ranks hooks, and profiles audience segments based on real post performance logs at the end of every session.
Install globally or in your project workspace with a single command:
npx skills add rabden/X-twitter-social-manager-skill
GitHub Repo: https://github.com/rabden/X-twitter-social-manager-skill
Let me know if you try it out or have any feedback! Always down to iterate on this! 🛠️

Yeah, the thing is I trust GPT's thinking to be accurate to what the model is at least considering doing. I will see GPT think about things and then not do it, but I do trust that GPT's thoughts are indicative of the sorts of actions it's thinking about taking. Whereas with Kimi, Mini Max and GLM, I have to turn thinking off because their thinking is just absolute it's sideways to their actions. With DeepSeek, I've noticed that the thinking is very reflective of the actions the model is going to take, but extremely so and it's similar to how it is with GPT where if it's come up in its thought, it's either going to act on it or it's considered it and you will see what it actually acts on in its thoughts later.

Some of the permissions, like the `external_directory` permission or the `task` permission, let you do more detailed permissions than just accept, deny, or ask.... `external_directory` lets you set permissions on specific directory, and Task lets you set permissions on specific subagents, for instance.

I don't specifically know about the TOS, but on a technical level, it should work with anything that supports the relevant type of endpoint that you are using for the model in question. As far as the TOS go, the important part is probably that you are not sharing it with other people.

Generally most of the Chinese models seem to prefer Anthropic endpoints, but not all of them. Some of them use OpenAI compatible endpoints. You would have to check which the model that you are interested in using uses.

I believe that for the models that are available on both, the prices should be the same. There are just some models that are only available on one or the other for reasons related mostly to where the hosting is located, or the price, etc.

Created. Using opencode in VS Code with deepseek V4 flash and Affinity start to finish 10 hours. Best experience on a Samsung ultra phone but can be used on any device desktop, tablet or phone on Android there is the ability to shake your phone to clear the canvas like
